title: Global poverty projections
subtitle: A fullScreen Scroller
source: Fullscreen scroller without particles (theme: light (alternatively: dark) World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform, World Bank Macro and Poverty Outlook October 2025, Mahler et al. (2025a), and Mahler et al. (2025b).

scenes.text: You can use the colors from the sidebar or use the Highlight color from the Google Docs menu with the color picker to highlight parts of your text and associate it with the visualization elements. If the color is too dark. make sure to change the text color to white too.
Yet copying Korea’s trajectory will be difficult. If countries manage to make progress at the typical speed observed since 1950, there will still be around 8% poverty by 2050.
Unfortunately, over the past years the speed of global poverty reduction has slowed down. If countries follow their current speed of progress, global poverty will soon start to increase.
